Skip NuGet publish steps for fork PRs (#1063)

Fork PRs don't have access to secrets or OIDC authentication,
causing the deploy job to fail. Add conditions to skip the
NuGet login and publish steps when the PR comes from a fork,
while keeping the deploy job itself green.

commit.message:
Enhance playtest report with per-agent roleplay action summary

- Added structured agent action tracking for DM, Lily, Thorne, and Valerius
- Captures key roleplay and gameplay actions during campaign simulation
- Prints concise post-run summary: what each agent did with their character
- Keeps test brief but preserves in-character roleplay progression
- Verified: playtest still passes with 0 issues

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update docs for playtest results, UX, and Mermaid diagrams

- Rewrote documentation.md to reflect current engine capabilities
- Added updated Mermaid diagrams for runtime sync, DM->Player flow,
  campaign UX journey, and playtest coverage/result
- Documented successful full-campaign multi-agent playtest (0 issues)
- Added DM quick-sheet sections for puzzle controls and audio/mood use
- Added campaign notes mapping acts to in-app puzzle flow

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add AI agent campaign playtest — full campaign simulation

Playwright test simulating the complete campaign with 4 AI agents:
  🎲 DM Agent — narrates scenes, runs puzzles, manages combat
  🗡️ Lily Agent — Rogue, uses Sneak Attack and Shortbow
  🛡️ Thorne Agent — Fighter, charges in with Longsword/Handaxe
  ✨ Valerius Agent — Paladin, Divine Smite, solves riddles

Full campaign flow tested:
  Act 1: Bakery — narration, spotlight puzzle (2/3 clues found), quest award
  Act 2: Woods — Hoot riddle puzzle (typewriter + hints + solve), combat
  Act 3: Peak — stepping stones puzzle (splash + victory), boss battle
  Finale: Sun-Cakes recovered, all 4 quests complete

Edge cases verified:
  - HP floor (can't go below 0) ✓
  - HP ceiling (can't exceed maxHp) ✓
  - Secret roll hidden from Player View ✓
  - Completed quests disabled ✓
  - Combat log populating ✓
  - State persistence across refresh ✓
  - Scene-specific monster filtering ✓

Result: 0 issues found — campaign plays perfectly!

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 3: Add three interactive campaign puzzles

- Bakery Spotlight Search: DM drags spotlight to find 3 hidden clues
  (flour paw prints, crumpled note, blue frosting smear). Player View
  shows dramatic darkness with spotlight cutout and animated reveals.

- Hoot's Riddle: Typewriter-style text reveal with DM-controlled pacing,
  3-tier hint system, and celebratory answer reveal. Full cinematic owl
  presentation with purple theme on Player View.

- Glimmer Stream Stepping Stones: Interactive puzzle where kids click
  stones on the Player View! Wrong stone = splash animation, right
  stone = green glow + advance. 4 rows of emoji stones. DM sees the
  safe path and can reset.

- Puzzle state syncs DM ↔ Player via BroadcastChannel (activePuzzle)
- DM sidebar auto-shows puzzle controls for current scene
- All 15 existing tests pass, lint clean, build succeeds

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Phase 2: Add immersive audio, particles, dice animation & action VFX

- Web Audio API synth engine (useAudio.js): per-scene ambient pads with
  3 mood variants (calm/tense/combat), crossfading on scene/mood change
- Sound effects: dice clatter, reveal tone, critical hit fanfare,
  critical fail descend, quest complete jingle
- DM audio controls: play/pause, volume slider, mood selector
  (calm/tense/combat) — mood syncs to Player View via BroadcastChannel
- CSS particle system (SceneEffects.jsx): bakery sparkles+steam,
  woods fireflies+leaves, peak snow+embers using Web Animations API
- Dice roll animation: number tumble with deceleration before reveal,
  synced with dice clatter SFX
- Action VFX: emoji-based overlays keyed to weapon/action type
  (sword slash, arrow, divine smite, magic, sneak attack)
- All 15 Playwright tests passing, lint clean, build succeeds

commit.message:
Confirm intent on trivially factual explicit invocations

Explicit invocation still bypasses the gate for genuine decisions,
but for questions with a single verifiable factual answer (e.g.
'what is the latest version of X') the agent should confirm intent
before spinning up a full debate.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Tighten debate process invocation gate

- Add explicit/proactive distinction: explicit user invocation always
  proceeds; dual gate only applies to proactive AI suggestions
- Require both a matching criterion AND meaningful cost/risk to justify
  overhead (prevents single loose criterion from firing the debate)
- Tighten 'previous fix' criterion to functional behavior, performance,
  or safety — exclude style, naming, formatting, minor behavioral nits
- Tighten 'pressure-test' criterion to require expensive-to-reverse or
  correctness/safety impact
- Add concrete 'Do not proactively suggest for' list targeting the
  middle band (cheap-to-reverse, obvious root cause, docs-answered,
  quickly-testable, style/naming)
- Update README snippet and process doc consistently

Reviewed by GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.6 before implementation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix ChecksumPreview print path: pre-render SVGs with physical mm dimensions

Previously the print window loaded JsBarcode from an external CDN and
rendered SVGs at pixel-only dimensions, so printed size was
browser-dependent and not at the configured physical dimensions.

Now matches BarcodePreview.tsx approach:
- Pre-render each barcode SVG using bundled JsBarcode (no CDN/network dep)
- Set viewBox + width/height in mm on each SVG before injection
- Inline SVGs in print window HTML (no script tags, no race condition)
- Add @page rule, per-card mm dimension label, and print-at-100% note
- Remove unused escapeForJsString helper

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix section icon styling in BatchGenerator to match Generate screen

Ruler, Package, and Maximize2 section header icons were using
text-muted-foreground (gray) instead of text-primary (red) with
the bg-primary/10 rounded container used consistently on the
Generate screen (BarcodeControls.tsx).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Replicant CAR V&V Validated Barcodes

V&V artifact set for the Replicant CAR project:
- 30 validated barcode PNGs (CODE128, CODE39, ITF, codabar)
- layout.json configuration
- validated barcodes.pdf compiled output

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Use full ISO timestamp in PDF export filename to prevent overwrites

Each Export PDF click now produces a uniquely named file
(e.g. batch_barcodes_2026-03-27_16-51-03.pdf) so multiple
exports in the same session are all retained.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Complete Metal.jl GPU acceleration

- Fix run_gpu! loop to follow exact 16-step CPU integration order
- Fix recovery duration: use lognormal (matching CPU default) instead of exponential
- Fix dynamic network support: re-generate edges each step (was using static cached edges)
- Add SEIR GPU support (exposed→infected transition, recovery time setting)
- Fix SIS GPU: use ti_recovered-based recovery, add immunity waning + boost
- Add cache_edges option for static network optimization
- Optimize results: GPU-side sum() instead of full state download
- GPUDiseaseArrays extended with exposed/ti_exposed fields
- Add _sample_recovery_durations helper matching CPU distribution
- Correctness verified: r>0.999 for SIR, SIS, SEIR (30 seeds)
- Update GPU vignette with actual benchmarks and working examples
- Extension now 1,696 lines (was 1,385)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: tryFromName() compares using $case->name

While fromName() compares using $case->getName(). Today these are equivalent, but the mismatch duplicates logic and can drift if getName() ever changes. Consider using the same comparison in both methods (or implement one in terms of the other) to keep behavior consistent.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Hugo Doueil <46538211+ormidales@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix critical security and logic bugs found in code review

Security Fixes:
- CRITICAL: Remove eval() for BUILD_CMD and TEST_CMD in patch_test.sh
  Replaced with bash -c to prevent command injection via env variables
- HIGH: Replace /tmp/build.log and /tmp/test.log with mktemp()
  Prevents race conditions and data corruption in multi-user systems

Logic & Validation Fixes:
- HIGH: Fix division by zero vulnerability in patch_check.sh calc_similarity()
  Added proper handling for zero adds/dels cases
- MEDIUM: Add input validation for silicon test results in patch_test.sh
  Only accepts PASS/FAIL/PENDING, defaults to PENDING on invalid input
- MEDIUM: Fix per-file diff stat calculation in patch_check.sh
  Use awk to track adds/dels per file instead of counting entire patch
- MEDIUM: Add validation for empty review branch in patch_check.sh
  Dies with clear error if branch name cannot be extracted from JSON
- LINUX-ONLY: Simplified stat command to use only Linux syntax (macOS support deferred)

All Python tests still pass (29/29 ✅)
All fixes tested for correctness

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Implementation details (helpers, JSON output, git integration)
- Testing results (Python tests pass, bash integration verified)
- Limitations and potential enhancements
- Performance comparison and maintenance notes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: external-patch-handling
repository.description: Python CLI pipeline for structured cross-company BIOS patch exchange (BHS). Covers receive, apply, functional equivalence check, test, review report, and integration.

commit.message:
Add bash versions of patch pipeline scripts

- patch_receive.sh: Validate and stage patches from a directory
- patch_apply.sh: Apply staged patches to a review branch
- patch_check.sh: Compare sender intent vs actual changes landed
- patch_test.sh: Run build and unit tests, collect silicon results
- patch_integrate.sh: Cherry-pick reviewed patches to working branch

All bash scripts:
- Provide zero-dependency alternatives to Python versions
- Generate compatible JSON output for downstream steps
- Support same configuration via .patch-pipeline.toml and env vars
- Can be mixed with Python scripts in the same pipeline
- Include comprehensive error handling and user-friendly output

Added BASH_README.md with detailed usage guide and examples.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
